message.content:
[
  {
    "idea": "Weekly surge in Google Trends searches for 'DIY home repair' signals consumer stress lifting XLY",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search interest",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "An uptick in DIY home repair searches often precedes increased discretionary spending on home improvement and related retail sectors.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Consumer discretionary spending tends to rise as home repair interest grows."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "Enter long XLY when 3-week moving average of 'DIY home repair' searches rises 15%+ versus prior 3 weeks",
      "exit": "Exit after 4 weeks or when the search interest drops below its 3-week moving average",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Weekly Google Trends data often shows multiple spikes per year in home repair interest."
  },
  {

      "type": "api",

    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Temporary job growth spikes reflect labor market caution that can depress discretionary spending."
    },

    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Weekly labor job postings regularly show sudden temp job surges around seasonal and business cycle events."
  },
